free_text: Measurements of the elastic scattering cross-section of
alpha particles of the heavy and medium weight nuclei
in the vicinity of the Coulomb barrier in a wide
angular range
- data only on figs.
- Full report with data tables
120 cm cyclotron
Collimated beam of alpha particles.
The exact energies of the incident alpha particles at
half target thickness are given.
Nb, Pd, Ag, In, Sn, Ta, Pt, Am, Pb, Bi self-supporting
targets were made of natural elements.
Three silicon surface barrier counters were
mounted on a rotatable arm inside the scattering
chamber.
After passing the target the beam was collected in
the Faraday cup connected with the current integrator.
The energy measurements
were verified kinematically, determining the angle at
which the alpha peaks from two independent sources
coincided.
The intensity of the beam was monitored by
semiconductor counter viewing the target at a fixed
angle of 20 degrees.
Faraday cup connected with the current
integrator.
The absolute values of cross-sections for all targets
except the lightest ones were determined by
normalization of the experimental data, taken at
relative small angles, to the Rutherford cross-section.
Corrections were made for energy loss in the targets.
The accuracy of the angle setting
Error of the absolute value of the cross-
section (compiler believes that this is error of
normalization)
The relative errors in the values of
the experimental differential cross-section vary
from 1% to 10 % in deep minima.
SD: Data of Rutherford ratio  were added
to Subents 002-011. Small corrections in all Subents.
STATUS updated in Subents 002-011.
41Nb target (rolled) was used. The Nb target  was
contaminated by a small percentage of tantalum. This
contamination was determined by chemical analysis.
Thickness was 3.70 mg/cm2.
For angles smaller than 90 degrees the contribution of
alpha particles scattered on tantalum was calculated
and subtracted.
Table 2 of INP-778,1971 report.

and data were added.  THICKNESS deleted.
